mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a robust economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential recessionary pressures, which typically leads to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is rising while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also accelerating, it signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle to combat inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL show significant 3-month increases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, translating into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon, particularly during periods of economic recovery.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods identified as post-shock recovery, despite positive INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer demand, which supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts indicates strong consumer and business confidence, signaling future economic activity and investment, which typically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods where the 3-month average of HOUST exceeds 1200 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are declining while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, it suggests stagflationary pressures or a consumer spending slowdown, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of periods characterized by declining retail sales and rising inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A rapidly increasing Federal Funds Rate signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampens economic growth, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon, especially during periods of high market volatility.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during periods of high market volatility when FEDFUNDS has increased by more than 0.5% over three months.
